title: Salisbury Steak
intro: Salisbury steak is classic American comfort food built from seasoned ground meat, a crisp browned crust, and gravy served on the plate instead of simmered into the patties. This version keeps that diner spirit while using a structured binder, a crust-friendly coating, and a crisp finish.

ingredientGroups.title: For Rehydrating the Mushrooms
items: 6 g dried porcini mushrooms
4 g dried shiitake mushrooms
Hot water or stock, enough to cover

ingredientGroups.title: For the Patty Binder
items: 1 large egg
1½ tbsp soy sauce
1 tsp Dijon mustard
1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
¾ cup panko breadcrumbs

ingredientGroups.title: For the Patty Mix
items: 16 oz Beyond Beef
Rehydrated porcini and shiitake, squeezed dry and minced very fine
1 medium yellow onion, chopped
1 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp cayenne pepper
1 tsp garlic powder
½ tsp sezonador total
1½ tbsp fresh parsley, minced
Fresh black pepper, to taste

ingredientGroups.title: For the Crust
items: 3 tbsp cornstarch
4½ tbsp cold water
1½ cups plain panko breadcrumbs
Light pinch black pepper

ingredientGroups.title: For Cooking and Serving
items: Olive oil, just enough to lightly coat the pan
Heinz jar gravy, warmed separately

stepGroups.title: Rehydrate and Prepare the Mushrooms
items: Cover the porcini and shiitake with hot water or stock.
Cover and steep 25 to 30 minutes.
Drain, squeeze very dry, and mince extremely fine until nearly paste-like.
Discard the soaking liquid.

stepGroups.title: Build the Patty Mixture
items: In a large bowl, whisk the egg, soy sauce, Dijon, and Worcestershire.
Add the panko and stir.
Rest 1 to 2 minutes so the crumbs hydrate.
Add Beyond Beef, the minced mushroom paste, chopped onion, smoked paprika, cayenne, garlic powder, sezonador total, parsley, and black pepper.
Mix gently just until cohesive. Do not knead.
Let the mixture rest 5 minutes in the refrigerator so the breadcrumbs fully hydrate and the patties hold together cleanly.

stepGroups.title: Form and Chill the Patties
items: Form 8 round patties, about 4 oz each.
Flatten slightly to about ¾ inch thick.
Keep the edges clean and thickness even.
Place the patties on a tray and freeze 30 minutes. This locks geometry, improves crust adhesion, and prevents spreading.

stepGroups.title: Coat the Patties
items: Whisk the cornstarch and cold water into a thin slurry.
Place the panko in a shallow dish and season lightly with black pepper.
Dip each chilled patty quickly in the slurry, coating both sides.
Let the excess drip off so only a thin film remains.
Press lightly into the panko to coat both sides and edges.
Return the coated patties to the tray and refrigerate 5 minutes. This helps the coating adhere and reduces crumb loss in the pan.

stepGroups.title: Cook and Serve
items: Warm the Heinz jar gravy gently in a small saucepan. Keep warm but do not boil.
Heat a cast-iron or stainless skillet over medium heat.
Add a thin film of olive oil.
Fry the patties 3 to 4 minutes per side, flipping once, until deeply golden and fully set.
Cook in batches if necessary and do not crowd the pan.
Plate the patties first.
Spoon hot gravy over the top only.
Do not simmer the crusted patties in gravy.
For the full TV dinner experience, serve with boxed instant mashed potatoes prepared with olive oil and skim milk instead of butter and whole milk.
Do not use nut milk for the potatoes. Even unsweetened nut milks tend to make the potatoes taste sweet.
